page: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine instance belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a high base frequency of 4.2 GHz and includes 260 MB of L3 cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 42.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage but is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals highly asymmetric performance characteristics. Single-core performance is a major strength, with the Geekbench single-core score reaching 2335.0, placing it in the top tier. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression (34.91 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (14.19 GB/sec) perform strongly. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is limited by the 2-vCPU configuration, resulting in weak multi-core scores across Geekbench (2876.0) and stress-ng (2215.99). Memory bandwidth is also relatively constrained, with large-block read bandwidth at 21.0 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 14.33 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the FX2ms_v2 offers a specialized resource profile that pairs a high-frequency, large-cache Intel processor with an exceptionally dense memory allocation per vCPU. This makes it highly suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallelization, such as small-scale in-memory databases, single-threaded data processing, and text processing tasks. It is less suited for highly parallelized compute-intensive applications, heavy multi-threaded compiling, or workloads requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a 2-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 42.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is premium storage capable. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2335.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak due to the 2-vCPU limit. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, single-threaded workloads, such as small in-memory databases, text processing, and lightweight caching layers, rather than highly parallelized compute tasks.

Equipped with 42.0 GB of RAM, offering 21.0 GB of memory per core
